title: Remote Transfer v2 maximum fan-out planning stress test
problem: AO Operator needs a conductable, high-task-count stress lane that exercises Remote Transfer v2 planning without spending live provider cycles or exposing network endpoints.
objective: Materialize a 2007-task AO Operator topology with 1000 Remote Transfer v2 implementation factories, 1000 reviewers, and durable SDD-style closure artifacts.
success_criteria: AO Operator materializes a 2007-task RunSpec and exact prompt set.
The contract declares 1000 disjoint implementation slices.
The topology validator accepts topology sizes above the original 17-task demo.
The dry-run completes without live provider execution.
constraints: Keep the stress lane dry-run by default.
Keep branch context bounded to scoped reads, writes, and durable artifacts.
Fan out only after Spec Forge and Ralph Loop gates pass.
negative_constraints: MUST NOT configure OPENAI_API_KEY or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
MUST NOT transfer provider auth files between machines
MUST NOT bind anonymous write-capable endpoints
MUST NOT run remote Codex from this dry-run stress lane
MUST NOT pass full transcripts between branches
MUST NOT accept closure when any branch returns BLOCKED or REJECTED

shalls.text: The stress topology SHALL contain planner-intake, Spec Forge, Ralph Loop, plan-hardener, factory-manager, 1000 implementation factories, 1000 reviewers, integrator, and evaluator-closer.
Every implementation factory SHALL declare disjoint read and write ownership.
Every reviewer SHALL depend only on its paired implementation factory.
The integrator SHALL fan in all 1000 reviewer artifacts.
The dry-run SHALL not require live provider execution or network endpoint exposure.

acceptance_criteria.description: The stress topology contains exactly 2007 tasks.
acceptance_criteria.oracle: RunSpec and topology validation report every task present.

acceptance_criteria.description: The contract carries all 1000 Remote Transfer v2 slices.
acceptance_criteria.oracle: Intake validation passes with reads, writes, verification, sensitive fields, trigger hints, and negative constraints.

acceptance_criteria.description: The dry-run materializes prompts and RunSpec without live provider dispatch.
acceptance_criteria.oracle: Factory run dry-run command exits 0 and generated status says mode dry-run.

verification: identity status projection acceptance documented
ready busy offline heartbeat states documented
timeout behavior keeps task state durable
enrollment failure modes remain typed
connection errors are structured
small bundles keep existing single-message path
large bundles use begin chunk commit semantics
retry resends only failed chunk index
failed uploads clean partial staging files
canonical manifest fields are named
missing and invalid signatures reject before metadata trust
key rotation grace period documented
provider-free marker smoke documented
artifact name size sha256 content return metadata documented
temp workspace cleanup behavior documented
Ubuntu local Codex auth preflight documented with workspace-provider-auth-material failure reason named
remote Codex smoke declared single write
task started artifact completed failed run terminal events and normalized reason documented without claiming provider progress persistence coverage
operator loop and stop rules documented
recovery matrix covers connection upload signing smoke and provider failures
worker discovery states and TTL boundaries documented
capability advertisements remain typed and versioned
remote queue lease ownership and expiry documented
remote task claim race behavior documented
remote task cancellation state transitions documented
backpressure states prevent overload
compression settings preserve deterministic hashes
delta bundle fallback behavior documented
chunk hash validation rejects corrupt uploads
resumable upload checkpoints documented
staging quota exhaustion behavior documented
manifest schema version handling documented
manifest compatibility fallback documented
signer policy allowlist behavior documented
trust store lookup and missing-key failure documented
audit log event coverage documented
artifact download retry behavior documented
artifact deduplication by digest documented
result metadata schema documented
cleanup sweeper intervals and failure handling documented
Codex environment probe remains local to Ubuntu
Codex transcript redaction policy documented
Codex timeout and retry policy documented
heartbeat event cadence documented
event replay idempotency documented
operator dashboard source signals documented
resume recovery path documented
rollback recovery path documented
tar sanitization checks documented
path normalization checks documented
